content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.

hints: For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Alternate Link Admission model · Theory Feed Ts…, name the reader, publisher, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, tenant, URL, unknown XML, runtime validation, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, PostgreSQL, Express, handle, accessibility, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Feed Ts Alternate Link Admission precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Alternate Link Admission model · Theory Feed Ts…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 6607 during a PostgreSQL lease and migration fault exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then change one emitted artifact, package, container, incident, backup, restore, rollback, or capacity condition and reconcile evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.

For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Defend the Feed Ts Alternate Link Admission model · Theory Feed Ts…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A link whose href contains feed is trustworthy discovery evidence.
